question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked zero didactic questions. It immediately wrote the full implementation, tested it, and explained the design — no choice points were surfaced to the user despite the system being designed around two-option didactic forks (e.g., HashMap+DLL vs OrderedDict, sentinel vs null-check, eviction policy timing).

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed at all. Notable forks like OrderedDict vs explicit DLL or update-then-evict ordering were mentioned only retrospectively in the explanation rather than offered as a decision.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no question sequence to scaffold. The Pair delivered a complete solution in one shot, so there is no progression or difficulty gradient to evaluate.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is clear and the post-hoc explanation is well-structured, but it's a monologue — the only 'user' turn is a Haiku-generated recap. There is no back-and-forth, which is the antithesis of the project's didactic two-option goal.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
